page: Hetzner Cloud cx33 is an x86_64 virtual machine powered by the QEMU hypervisor. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, meaning resources are multiplexed with other tenants. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 1.95 GB per core. Local storage is provided via an 80 GB SSD, and there are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included in the base configuration.

Performance benchmarks show a strong single-core CPU capability, with stress-ng single-core performance ranking in the top 25%. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic operations fall within the average range. However, memory-intensive tasks show limitations, as evidenced by a weak PassMark memory score and weak performance in software compilation with Clang. Static web serving throughput is also positioned in the bottom 25% of tested configurations.

Given its shared CPU architecture and budget-friendly profile, the cx33 offers a cost-effective option for workloads that do not require dedicated CPU threads. It is well-suited for light background processing, development environments, basic text processing, and small-scale LLM text generation. It is less suitable for memory-bound applications, heavy software compilation, or high-throughput web serving.
description: Hetzner Cloud cx33 is an x86_64 virtual server configured with 4 shared vCPUs on an AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz. It includes 8.0 GB of RAM and 80 GB of local SSD storage, operating under the QEMU hypervisor. While single-core CPU benchmarks show strong performance in the top 25%, multi-core execution and memory bandwidth remain average. The instance exhibits weaker performance in memory-heavy tasks, software compilation, and static web serving. Lacking dedicated CPU allocation and complimentary public IPv4 addresses, this budget-friendly server is best suited for development environments, light background tasks, and low-concurrency applications where cost efficiency is prioritized over sustained high-performance multi-core execution.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cx33 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and an 80 GB local SSD, offering a budget-friendly option for development and light workloads.

tagline: A budget-friendly shared-CPU virtual server featuring AMD EPYC processors and local SSD storage for light workloads and development environments.

8.0 GB of system memory and 80 GB of local SSD storage
Shared CPU allocation suitable for budget-conscious hosting
Best suited for development, light background tasks, and basic text processing
